Title: Auto-generated uniform controls
Summary: Every uniform you declare gets a UI control derived from its type + hint annotation: sliders for hint_range, native color pickers for source_color, grouped number inputs otherwise.
Body:
## What you get for each declaration

| Declaration                                            | Control                                          |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `uniform bool name;`                                   | Checkbox                                         |
| `uniform int name : hint_range(min, max[, step]);`     | Integer slider with min/max/step                 |
| `uniform float name : hint_range(min, max[, step]);`   | Float slider with min/max/step                   |
| `uniform int name;` / `uniform float name;`            | Plain number input                               |
| `uniform vec3 name : source_color;`                    | Native browser color picker                      |
| `uniform vec4 name : source_color;`                    | Color picker + separate alpha slider             |
| `uniform vec2/3/4 name;` (no hint)                     | Grouped number inputs, one per component         |

`hint_color` is treated as an alias for `source_color` — same control either way.

## Default values

The default value from the declaration (`= 0.5`, `= vec3(1.0, 0.5, 0.0)`, `= true`) seeds the initial control state. `vec(...)` literals are parsed component-by-component; `vec3(0.5)` expands to `[0.5, 0.5, 0.5]` (Godot's broadcast shorthand).

If the declaration has `hint_range` but no `=`, the slider seeds at the range's `min`. Without either, the control starts at type-appropriate zero (0 / false / [0, 0, 0]).

## Preservation across edits

When you change the source — add a new uniform, rename one, change a hint — the panel re-derives its shape on the next parse. Tweaked values are **preserved across the change** when a uniform's name *and* type both still match. If you add a brand-new uniform, it seeds from its default. If you rename one, the old name's value drops and the new name starts from default.

In practice this means you can scrub a slider value, then go back to the editor and add a new uniform, and your scrubbed value doesn't reset.

## Reset

The **Reset uniforms** link at the bottom of the controls reseeds every uniform from its current declaration's default. Doesn't reload from disk; doesn't touch the source. Just the in-memory values.

## Limitations

- Extra `sampler2D` uniforms beyond the built-in `TEXTURE` aren't supported — the translator rejects them at parse time with a specific message. See [the unsupported pane entry](/help/shaders/unsupported-pane).
- `mat2/3/4` uniforms aren't in the v1 subset either. If you need matrices in a shader you preview live, you'd be coming from a `spatial` context anyway, which has its own subset blockers — see [the translator subset](/help/shaders/translator-subset).
- Custom hint types (`hint_default_white`, `hint_normal`, etc.) on the supported scalar/vector types are rejected — they're 3D / sampler-affecting hints that don't apply to the canvas_item live preview.
